summary: Pass cost as parameter to handle_job_result to avoid double get_job_cost call
reasoning: The abort path was calling get_job_cost twice: once at line 2118 which stops the RateLimiter, then again at line 2345 in the abort handler which finds a dead process and returns 0.0. Fixed by passing the cost as a parameter to handle_job_result so it can be reused in the abort handler.

summary: Implemented abort_job/1 with full cleanup logic per spec Section 5
reasoning: Added Lead worktree removal, job branch deletion (respecting keep_failed_branches config), and original branch restoration. Follows patterns from complete_job/1 and cleanup_orphans/1. Uses force delete (-D) for job branch since uncommitted work is intentionally discarded on abort.

summary: Created abort_job/1 function to pop stash on job failure/abort
reasoning: Added minimal abort_job/1 that calls pop_job_stash to restore user's stashed changes when a job fails or is aborted. This prevents user changes from being permanently stranded. Full cleanup (worktree removal, branch deletion per Section 5) will be added in a future iteration per second work item.

summary: Added started_site_log flag to track Store ownership in Foreman
reasoning: When Foreman starts Store directly (in tests/resume), it needs cleanup on termination. Used boolean flag instead of checking process links to make ownership explicit and survive across state transitions.

summary: Fixed merge-resolution retry counter to increment on each attempt
reasoning: handle_merge_retry_attempt was passing retry_count unchanged to handle_lead_merge_with_retry, causing the retry counter to never increment. This prevented the retry cap check (retry_count >= max_retries) from ever triggering, allowing infinite merge-resolution loops. Fixed by passing retry_count + 1 instead of retry_count at foreman.ex:2358.

summary: Fixed RateLimiter.reconcile/4 to handle nil actual_usage
reasoning: Runner's collect_stream_events initializes usage = nil (runner.ex:265); if no Usage events arrive from the provider, RateLimiter.reconcile is called with nil; Map.get(nil, :input, ...) raises BadMapError, crashing the RateLimiter GenServer. Fixed by checking if actual_usage is nil: when nil, use estimated_tokens (no credit) and skip track_cost (no actual usage data). When actual_usage is a map, use actual input tokens and track cost normally.

summary: Fixed Foreman call_llm to pass read-only tools to provider
reasoning: Line 1518 hardcoded tools=[] but execute_tool at line 1463 defines [Read, Grep, Find, Ls]. The provider never received tool definitions, so the LLM couldn't generate tool calls. This prevented the Foreman from reading the codebase during planning and decomposition phases. Fixed by changing tools=[] to the read-only tools list that matches execute_tool's expectations.

summary: Fixed calibrate_from_usage to use String.length instead of byte_size
reasoning: Multi-byte characters (emoji priority markers 🔴🟡🟢) inflate byte count, causing calibration factor drift. Tokens.estimate/2 uses String.length for character count, so calibration must match to maintain consistency.

summary: Fixed edit tool line-range deletion to cleanly delete when new_content is empty
reasoning:
String.split("", "
") returns [""] not [], causing spurious blank line. Fixed by checking for empty string explicitly. Also updated test expectation from buggy behavior to correct behavior.

summary: Refactored bash tool to stream-truncate output instead of buffering in memory
reasoning: The original implementation accumulated all command output in memory via string concatenation before truncation, which could cause OOM for large outputs. Fixed by: (1) removing in-memory accumulation during collection, (2) streaming only to temp file, (3) reading back only the tail (last 30KB) from the file after command completes. Also extracted helper functions to reduce cyclomatic complexity.

summary: Fixed inject_skill handler to set turn_count: 1 on transition to :calling
reasoning: The inject_skill handler at lines 289-296 was missing turn_count initialization that the regular prompt path sets at line 235; spec v0.2 changelog clarifies turn counter must include the initial prompt-triggered LLM call; this caused skill-triggered conversations to get one extra turn before the limit fires; fix: added turn_count: 1 to the new_data map in inject_skill handler

summary: Fixed safety pass-rate extraction to use final result line instead of first match
reasoning: Lines 67 and 79 used 'head -1' which captures the first matching line; if intermediate log output contains parenthesized numbers (e.g., iteration count), wrong value extracted and threshold check bypassed; spec 3.2 requires safety evals (hallucination, PII) below 90% to hard fail; fix: changed 'head -1' to 'tail -1' on both lines to extract the final result line

summary: Removed conditional from safety eval check in CI to ensure unconditional execution
reasoning: The 'Check for safety eval failures' step had `if: steps.evals.outputs.exit_code != '0'` which skipped the safety threshold check when make test.eval exits 0; spec 3.2 requires safety evals (hallucination, PII) below 90% to hard fail the build unconditionally; fix: removed the conditional so safety check always runs and parses test output

summary: Added epoch staleness check to immediate reflection path
reasoning: Immediate reflection path (non-buffered) was applying reflection results without verifying activation_epoch. Buffered path already had this check per spec 6.4. Fix: (1) modified spawn_reflector_task to capture and tag result.epoch like spawn_buffered_reflector_task does, (2) extracted apply_immediate_reflection_with_epoch_check helper to check epoch before applying (reduces nesting per Credo), (3) discards stale reflection results with warning log, clearing is_reflecting flag

summary: Fixed om_snapshot fallback to use observation from main JSONL when snapshot file missing
reasoning: load_latest_snapshot returns {:ok, nil} when file doesn't exist, not {:error, _}; previous code matched {:ok, snapshot} which set om_snapshot=nil when file missing, never falling back to state.om_state from main JSONL; spec v0.4 requires using observation entries as fallback; fix: match {:ok, nil} separately to return state.om_state, return nil on actual read errors to start fresh safely

summary: Promoted epoch staleness check finding to work item
reasoning: immediate (non-buffered) reflection path applies results without checking activation_epoch; buffered path has this check; spec 6.4 requires discarding stale results; verification that is_reflecting prevents new activations followed by fix needed

summary: Promoted type mismatch finding to work item
reasoning: resume/2 fallback uses %Entry.Observation{} from find_latest_observation but OM.Supervisor may expect OM state struct; if pattern-matches on specific struct, fallback crashes on resume; spec clarified in v0.4 to use observation entries as fallback but type compatibility must be verified

summary: Confirmed: double get_job_cost in abort path reports $0.00
reasoning: run_work_on_issue calls get_job_cost at line 2118 which stops the RateLimiter; handle_job_result {:error, :aborted} calls it again at line 2345; second call finds dead process, returns 0.0. Regression from v0.5 handle_job_result implementation.

summary: Rejected: cycle detection misses members
reasoning: Traced DFS algorithm through multiple scenarios (A->B->C->A, D->A->B->C->A, E->B with prior cycle). All cases correctly identify cycle members. Agent analysis was wrong about visited-node cutoff behavior.

summary: Confirmed: stash not popped on job failure/abort
reasoning: pop_job_stash exists and is called in complete_job (success path) per history fix. No abort_job or failure cleanup path calls it. User changes permanently stranded on failed jobs.

summary: Confirmed: abort_job missing entirely
reasoning: No abort_job function exists in git/job.ex. keep_failed_branches config not read anywhere. Spec Section 5 failure cleanup is unimplemented.

summary: Rejected: merge conflict detection too narrow
reasoning: git merge exit code 1 is the standard for conflicts. Exit 128 is fatal errors (not conflicts). The exit_code==1 && CONFLICT check is the correct pattern for git.

summary: Confirmed: Foreman tools=[] blinds planning
reasoning: call_llm at line 1518 hardcodes tools=[] while execute_tool at line 1463 defines Read/Grep/Find/Ls. Provider never receives tool definitions so LLM never generates tool calls. Foreman cannot read codebase during planning.

summary: Confirmed: RateLimiter crashes on nil usage
reasoning: Runner usage initialized to nil at line 265. If no Usage events in stream, nil passed to reconcile. Map.get(nil, :input, ...) raises BadMapError, killing RateLimiter for entire job.

summary: Confirmed: merge retry counter never increments
reasoning: handle_merge_retry_attempt at line 2358 passes retry_count unchanged. Should be retry_count+1. The max 3 retry cap added in git-strategy v0.2 is non-functional.

summary: Confirmed: Foreman missing terminate callback
reasoning: No terminate/3 in foreman.ex. In isolated startup (test, resume), Store started directly without supervisor. DETS not flushed on Foreman exit, risking corruption.

summary: Confirmed: calibrate_from_usage uses byte_size not String.length
reasoning: state.ex:1445 uses byte_size(content_text). Spec v0.3 requires String.length. Tokens.estimate was already fixed per history but calibration path was missed. Multi-byte emoji inflate factor.

summary: Sent to review: immediate reflection epoch check
reasoning: Non-buffered reflection path at state.ex:687-731 applies results without epoch check. Buffered path checks. Unclear if is_reflecting:true prevents concurrent activations. Needs human judgment.

summary: Rejected: keep_tail applies only to observed messages
reasoning: Unobserved messages MUST be in context because the Observer needs to see them. The tail budget correctly applies only to already-observed messages. Including all unobserved is required for the OM pipeline to function.

summary: Rejected: om_hard_threshold_multiplier not wired in state.ex
reasoning: The multiplier IS wired in agent/context.ex at lines 143 and 159, which is the correct location. The sync fallback checks happen in the Agent context assembly, not the OM state machine.

summary: Marking Implemented: no findings
reasoning: use_skill returning tool result is a valid approach (code works), and load_definition nil concern is a defensive programming issue not a practical functional break. All spec requirements met.

summary: Marking Implemented: no findings
reasoning: Task.async+unlink is a valid workaround for non-existent Task.async_nolink (without supervisor). System realpath is valid workaround for non-existent File.realpath/1. Both choices documented in decisions log with reasoning.

summary: Marking Implemented: no findings
reasoning: Link without href produces empty parens — cosmetic, spec only requires no crash. Store.list/1 vs /0 — spec wording imprecise but code is consistent. All spec requirements functionally met.

summary: Marking Implemented: no findings
reasoning: message_start emitting Usage is correct behavior (spec table incomplete but code is right). parse_event tool_use branch is dead code in streaming path but streaming works correctly. No functional breaks.

summary: Rejected: reconstruct_messages drops ToolResult
reasoning: History line 383 shows this was a known issue already fixed. Tool results are now embedded in Message entries by the harness. The ToolResult filter is intentional — Entry.ToolResult is metadata-only (timing info).

reasoning: tier1-evals.yml line 56: if steps.evals.outputs.exit_code != 0. When make test.eval exits 0, safety check step is entirely skipped. Safety eval below 90% passes CI silently.

summary: Confirmed: safety pass-rate extraction fragile with head -1
reasoning: grep -i hallucination | head -1 captures first matching line. If intermediate log output contains parenthesized numbers before the final result, wrong value is extracted.

summary: Confirmed: inject_skill missing turn_count initialization
reasoning: Regular prompt path sets turn_count:1 at line 235. inject_skill handler at lines 289-296 does not set turn_count. Skill-triggered conversations get one extra turn before limit fires.

summary: Rejected: abort nil tool_runner leaves tasks running
reasoning: get_tool_runner_supervisor was wired up per history line 408. The nil case only occurs if the session supervisor itself is dead, in which case the agent process would also be dead. Not a practical concern.

summary: Confirmed: bash tool buffers all output before truncating
reasoning: collect_output at bash.ex:126 does new_acc = acc <> data for all chunks. Truncation happens only after collection. Large output (hundreds of MB) will OOM the agent process.

summary: Confirmed: edit line-range empty content inserts blank line
reasoning:
String.split("", "
") returns [""] not []. Replacing a range with new_content:"" inserts a spurious blank line. Test at edit_test.exs:203 asserts the broken behavior.

summary: Rejected: edit diff missing hunk headers
reasoning: The diff output conveys correct change information to the LLM consumer. Missing @@ headers is a format choice, not a functional break. The agent can understand the changes.

summary: Rejected: grep context off-by-one
reasoning: Traced the math: line_num is 1-indexed, start_idx is 0-indexed. For line_num=5, num_lines=2: start_idx=max(0,5-2-1)=2, end_idx=min(len,5+2)=7. Enum.slice(2,5) gives indices 2-6 = lines 3-7. Correct 2 lines before, match, 2 lines after.

summary: Added explicit handle_job_result({:error, :aborted}, ...) clause for non-SIGINT aborts
reasoning: Non-SIGINT Foreman aborts (e.g., :shutdown exit) produce {:error, :aborted} from wait_for_job_completion. Without an explicit handler, this falls through to the generic {:error, reason} handler which calls exit({:shutdown, 1}), terminating the process. Added a new clause that rolls back issue status to :open, reports job cost, and returns {:error, :aborted} to allow the work loop to handle it gracefully. This matches the pattern of SIGINT handlers but adds cost reporting since the job has already incurred costs.

summary: Changed resolve_real_path to use system realpath command instead of :file.read_link_all/1
reasoning: :file.read_link_all/1 only resolves the final path component; if an intermediate directory is a symlink (e.g., /home/nick → /Users/nick), the symlink is not resolved, causing two paths to the same repo to produce different project directories and silo sessions/cache. File.realpath/1 doesn't exist in Elixir 1.19.5, so using System.cmd to call system realpath utility which resolves all symlinks in the path.

summary: Changed Store async load to use Task.async + Process.unlink instead of linked task
reasoning: Task.async_nolink/1 does not exist in Elixir; used Task.async followed by immediate Process.unlink to achieve monitored-but-not-linked behavior; this preserves the {:DOWN, ...} handler at line 252 for graceful error handling while preventing task crashes from killing the Store GenServer; if the load task crashes (e.g., DETS iteration error), the GenServer survives and returns :miss for not-yet-loaded entries

summary: Implemented safety eval hard-fail gate in CI workflow
reasoning: Parses test output for hallucination and PII eval pass rates using grep and pattern matching; hard fails build if either category drops below 90% threshold per spec section 3.2; gracefully handles case where no safety evals exist yet (no false failures); uses POSIX-compatible bash constructs for GitHub Actions compatibility

summary: Added retry cap (max 3 attempts) for merge-resolution Runner to prevent infinite loops
reasoning: Modified handle_merge_conflict to accept retry_count parameter and track it in merge-resolution task context. When merge-resolution Runner succeeds but handle_lead_merge still returns :conflict (indicating unresolved conflicts), the retry_count is checked before spawning another Runner. After max_retries (3), the merge fails with critical_finding and the Lead is removed from tracking. This prevents infinite loops when conflicts cannot be automatically resolved, per spec section 3 requirement.

summary: Added git stash pop after job completion to restore user's working state
reasoning: Modified perform_stash to use 'git stash push -m' with message 'Deft job creation: <job_id>' to identify stashes created during job creation. Added pop_job_stash function that searches for stash by message and pops it after successful job completion in complete_job. Threaded job_id through verify_clean_working_tree, handle_dirty_working_tree, handle_stash_response, and perform_stash. If pop fails, logs warning but doesn't fail job completion. This restores the user's uncommitted changes that were stashed before job creation, per spec section 1 requirement.

summary: Wired OM threshold config fields through Deft.Config by adding 6 new fields and updating all usages
reasoning: Added om_message_token_threshold, om_observation_token_threshold, om_buffer_interval, om_buffer_tail_retention, om_hard_threshold_multiplier, and om_previous_observer_tokens to Config struct. Replaced module attributes in state.ex with helper functions that read from config. For OM.Context (which doesn't have direct config access), chose to pass threshold values as optional parameters rather than passing entire config struct, maintaining module interface simplicity while allowing configuration.

summary: Fixed keep_tail/3 to skip oversized messages instead of halting
reasoning: Changed from Enum.reduce_while to Enum.reduce at context.ex:109-124 to continue processing when a message exceeds the token budget; previously halted on first oversized message, which caused zero messages to be kept if the most recent message was large; now skips oversized messages and continues to older messages, preserving conversational continuity per spec v0.3 requirement

summary: Fixed Tokens.estimate/2 to use String.length instead of byte_size
reasoning: Changed from byte_size to String.length at tokens.ex:26 to correctly count characters rather than bytes; multi-byte UTF-8 characters (emoji priority markers 🔴🟡🟢 are 4 bytes each) were inflating token estimates by up to 4x, causing observation/reflection cycles to trigger prematurely; spec v0.3 clarified that token estimation must use character count

summary: Fixed turn counter off-by-one by starting at 1 and changing check to >
reasoning: Changed turn_count initialization from 0 to 1 at agent.ex:235 and agent.ex:1048 (prompt and queued message handlers) to count the initial LLM call as turn 1; changed condition from >= to > at agent.ex:1439 so that with max_turns=25, exactly 25 LLM calls are made (turn_count 1-25) instead of the semantically incorrect 0-24 range; spec v0.2 clarified that turn counter must include the initial prompt-triggered LLM call in the count

summary: Fixed om_enabled default in maybe_compact_messages to true
reasoning: Changed default from false to true to match all other call sites (agent.ex:691, context.ex:90, config.ex:321); with a bare-map config, false default caused compaction to run alongside OM, violating the mutual-exclusion invariant that compaction should only run when OM is disabled

summary: Fixed abort in :executing_tools to terminate all Task.Supervisor children
reasoning: Changed cancel_state_operations to use Task.Supervisor.children + terminate_child for each PID instead of only terminating tracked tasks in data.tool_tasks; inner per-tool tasks spawned by ToolRunner.execute_batch were not tracked, so they continued running up to 120s after abort; now all tasks under the supervisor are terminated immediately per spec requirement

summary: Fixed resume/1 to use observation entries from main JSONL as fallback when _om.jsonl is missing
reasoning: Changed resume/1 to fall back to om_state reconstructed from observation entries in main JSONL when OMState.load_latest_snapshot fails; previously set om_snapshot to nil, losing OM state; now uses state.om_state as fallback, ensuring OM state is preserved from observation entries when snapshot file is absent

summary: Wired Store and OM.State to use project-scoped paths via Deft.Project.sessions_dir/1
reasoning: Store functions (append, load, resume, list) and OM.State snapshot functions now accept working_dir parameter and use Deft.Project.sessions_dir/1 instead of hardcoded ~/.deft/sessions; ensures sessions are project-scoped per spec v0.3; updated all callers in cli.ex, agent.ex, and session_picker.ex to pass working_dir from config; fixed tests to use project-scoped paths

summary: Wired Store and OM.State to use project-scoped paths via Deft.Project.sessions_dir/1
reasoning: Store functions (append, load, resume, list) and OM.State snapshot functions now accept working_dir parameter and use Deft.Project.sessions_dir/1 instead of hardcoded ~/.deft/sessions; ensures sessions are project-scoped per spec v0.3; updated all callers in cli.ex, agent.ex, and session_picker.ex to pass working_dir from config; fixed tests to use project-scoped paths

summary: Fixed link rendering crash for missing href attribute
reasoning: List.keyfind returns nil when href is absent, causing elem(nil, 1) to raise ArgumentError; added case statement to handle nil with empty string default

summary: Fixed fd exit code 1 handling in find tool
reasoning: fd v8+ uses exit code 0 for all successful operations (including zero results) and exit code 1 for actual errors (bad patterns, invalid paths, etc.). Previous code treated exit code 1 as 'no matches found', silently swallowing real errors. Changed to check for empty output on exit code 0 to distinguish between success-with-results and success-with-no-results, while treating all non-zero exit codes as errors.

summary: Replaced positional diff with LCS-based algorithm in edit tool
reasoning: Previous find_changes used simple positional line comparison (line i vs line i), causing all subsequent lines after an insert/delete to appear as changed. Implemented proper LCS (Longest Common Subsequence) algorithm with dynamic programming table that correctly identifies insertions, deletions, and context lines regardless of position shifts.

summary: Fixed Usage event accumulation in Runner collect_loop
reasoning: Anthropic sends separate message_start (input tokens) and message_delta (output tokens) events; replacing instead of accumulating caused final usage to have input: 0 and incorrect cost tracking. Changed to accumulate tokens across multiple Usage events.

summary: Add RateLimiter.reconcile calls in Foreman and Lead finalize_streaming
reasoning: Both Foreman and Lead request tokens via RateLimiter.request but never credited back the difference between estimated and actual usage. Modified call_llm to store estimated_tokens in data, then call RateLimiter.reconcile in finalize_streaming after LLM response completes. This ensures TPM bucket credits are properly refunded.

summary: Fixed Lead state_enter to use gen_statem:cast instead of next_event
reasoning: next_event is a transition_action prohibited in state_enter callbacks by OTP gen_statem. Replaced {:keep_state_and_data, [{:next_event, :cast, ...}]} with :gen_statem.cast(self(), ...) followed by :keep_state_and_data, matching the pattern used in Foreman

summary: Implemented Foreman execute_tool delegation to tool modules
reasoning: Followed Lead's pattern: built tool_context with working_dir/session_id/lead_id='foreman', provided read-only tools (Read/Grep/Find/Ls), built tool_map and dispatched to tool_module.execute with try/rescue. Added build_tool_context helper mirroring Lead's implementation. Foreman uses nil cache_tid (no cache during orchestration), bash_timeout 120s, emit no-op function.

summary: Demoted harness to Ready — 3 findings: abort inner tasks, om_enabled default, turn count off-by-one
reasoning: (1) agent.ex:1079 cancel_state_operations only terminates outer wrapper task; inner per-tool tasks under same supervisor continue running up to 120s after abort. (2) agent.ex:1540 defaults om_enabled to false but all other paths default true — bare-map config triggers compaction alongside OM. (3) agent.ex:1430 initial LLM call not counted; with max_turns=25, 26 calls happen. Previous fix (history line 384) changed > to >= but didn't count initial call. Rejected: continue_turn race (benign silent drop), stream error retry (correct behavior).

summary: Demoted observational-memory to Ready — 3 findings: byte_size vs String.length, keep_tail halt, OM config unwired
reasoning: (1) tokens.ex:26 uses byte_size not String.length; emoji markers (4 bytes each) inflate estimates up to 4x, causing premature observation/reflection triggers. (2) context.ex:117 reduce_while halts on first oversized message instead of skipping; single large tool result drops all tail messages. (3) All 6 OM config fields from spec section 8 are hardcoded module constants, not wired through Config. Rejected: CORRECTION marker section migration (complex/theoretical, hard to verify), sync fallback lag (one-turn delay, minor).

summary: Demoted git-strategy to Ready — 2 findings: stash never popped, merge-resolution retry unbounded
reasoning: (1) git/job.ex stashes user changes on request but never pops after complete_job — user's working state not restored. (2) foreman.ex:1000 merge-resolution Runner respawns on repeated conflict with no cap — potential infinite loop. Rejected: verify_no_worktrees ordering (normal path cleans up worktrees before complete_job), merge resolution handler state-lock (theoretical race, Foreman stays in :executing during merges).

summary: Demoted evals to Ready — 1 finding: safety eval hard-fail gate is a TODO stub
reasoning: tier1-evals.yml:64 has '# TODO: Parse test output for safety eval pass rates and hard fail if < 90%' and exits 0 unconditionally. History line 182 marks this as done but the implementation is incomplete — just a warning message. Rejected all other findings: component eval tests missing (already in history line 107), make test.eval zero tests (consequence of missing eval tests), scoring.ex location (infrastructure), eval_helpers.ex location (non-breaking), JudgeCalibration in lib (non-breaking), eval.compare key mismatch (modules not unified), baselines history unbounded (spec omission, not implementation bug).

summary: Demoted filesystem to Ready — 2 findings: Task.async linked crash, resolve_real_path incomplete
reasoning: (1) store.ex:177 uses Task.async (linked) for DETS load. If task crashes, linked exit kills Store GenServer before {:DOWN} handler fires. Should use Task.async_nolink for graceful degradation. (2) project.ex:129 uses :file.read_link_all/1 which only resolves final component symlinks; intermediate directory symlinks not resolved; File.realpath/1 handles all cases. History line 72 listed both options; chosen approach insufficient. Rejected: cleanup race (edge case during shutdown), task message shape (spec documentation issue, code works correctly).

summary: Demoted issues to Ready — 1 finding: non-SIGINT abort handler missing
reasoning: cli.ex:2304 generic handle_job_result({:error, reason}) calls exit({:shutdown, 1}) for all errors including :aborted. Non-SIGINT Foreman aborts (e.g., plan rejection causing :shutdown exit) produce {:error, :aborted} which hits this catch-all. The work loop's graceful abort branch (print message, report cost, return :ok) is unreachable. Rejected: cycle detection off-by-one (MapSet dedup makes it harmless), stale data query (two-step approach is correct), tool name heuristic (fallback only, confirmation uses real name), Jason keys:atoms (theoretical atom exhaustion, not current breakage).

summary: Demoted orchestration to Ready — 3 findings: Foreman execute_tool placeholder, Lead state_enter next_event crash, Foreman/Lead missing RateLimiter.reconcile
reasoning: Research agent returned 7 candidates; validated each against source code. Confirmed 3: (1) foreman.ex:1432 returns 'Tool result placeholder' for all tool calls — not in history, (2) lead.ex:232 uses {:next_event, :cast, ...} in state_enter which is prohibited by OTP gen_statem — crashes Lead on start, (3) Foreman/Lead call_llm never calls RateLimiter.reconcile — TPM bucket progressively drained. Rejected 4: verification handler state-lock (Foreman stays in :verifying,:idle), lead_pid nil (supervisor blocks until child starts), stale data in phase transition (data is correctly updated), research handler state (no LLM calls during research).

summary: Demoted providers to Ready — 1 finding: Runner.collect_loop replaces Usage events instead of accumulating
reasoning: Research agent found Runner.collect_loop at runner.ex:318 does blind assignment of usage instead of accumulation. Anthropic sends input tokens in message_start and output tokens in message_delta as separate events. Final usage always has input:0, breaking RateLimiter.reconcile (credits back full estimated tokens) and cost tracking (input cost always $0). Confirmed against actual code. Rejected finding about empty args_json — Anthropic always sends at least {} for no-arg tools.

summary: Demoted tools to Ready — 2 findings: edit diff algorithm wrong, find fd exit code handling wrong
reasoning: (1) edit.ex:295 find_changes does positional line comparison, not LCS/Myers. For edits that change line count, all subsequent lines appear as spurious delete+add pairs. Spec requires unified diff. (2) find.ex:96 treats fd exit code 1 as 'no results' but fd v8+ uses 1 for errors and 0 for success including zero results. Real errors silently swallowed. Rejected 5 other agent findings as non-functional: schema validation (errors correctly), bash memory (performance not correctness), grep overshoot (minor boundary), rg no max-count (performance), Task.yield_many (concurrent tasks get full wall time).

summary: Demoted tui to Ready — 2 findings: slash command CaseClauseError, markdown link crash
reasoning: (1) chat.ex:785 handle_slash_command case only handles :not_found and :no_definition but SlashCommand.dispatch can return {:error, reason, name} for I/O errors — CaseClauseError crashes Breeze view. (2) markdown.ex:244 calls elem(nil, 1) when link has no href attribute — ArgumentError crash. Rejected: /plan missing (owned by orchestration spec, not TUI), cosmetic glyph difference (○ vs ◉), streaming buffer (functionally correct despite re-parsing), session_picker nil guard (guarded at store level).

summary: Demoted sessions to Ready — 2 findings: flat storage path, OM state discarded on resume
reasoning: (1) store.ex:22 hardcodes @sessions_dir to ~/.deft/sessions but spec v0.3 requires project-scoped ~/.deft/projects/<encoded>/sessions/. Project module has sessions_dir/1 but Store never calls it. (2) store.ex:143-165 resume/1 loads om_state from observation entries via reconstruct_state but immediately overwrites with _om.jsonl snapshot. If snapshot missing, om_snapshot is nil and OM state lost even when valid observation entries exist. Rejected: dead code (not functional), String.to_atom (theoretical exhaustion not current breakage), CLI om_snapshot forwarding (borderline, covered by store path fix).

summary: Demoted skills to Ready — 1 finding: CLI injects skills as user messages instead of system instructions
reasoning: cli.ex:987-995 returns skill definition via {:ok, text} which goes to Deft.Agent.prompt/2 (user message). Spec section 2.4 requires system-level injection. TUI correctly uses {:inject_skill, full_text} at chat.ex:792-796. CLI has a TODO comment acknowledging this bug. The fix (skills v0.2 history line 207) was applied to TUI but not CLI.

summary: Confirmed rate-limiter v0.1 as Implemented — no new findings
reasoning: Research agent returned 2 candidate findings; both rejected. (1) Starvation FIFO: Enum.split_with preserves gb_trees key order; within same original priority, FIFO maintained. Cross-priority ordering after promotion is reasonable — spec doesn't define merge order for promoted requests. (2) Scale-up Enum.any? with multiple providers: system has single provider (Anthropic); behavior reasonable even with multiple. No action needed.

summary: Confirmed standards v0.1 as Implemented — no new findings
reasoning: Research agent returned 6 candidates; all rejected. (1) No eval tests — covered by evals spec, already in history. (2) No test/integration/ dir — pragmatic, no integration tests exist. (3) lefthook skip:true — intentional while prerequisites missing. (4) CI target combines eval+unit — no eval tests exist to block. (5) No property tests — covered by evals spec. (6) req_llm extra dependency — additional deps not spec-violating. All deviations are pragmatic accommodations for missing prerequisites, not wrong behavior.

summary: Marked orchestration v0.4 as Implemented — no new findings
reasoning: Audited all 10 spec sections against code. Research agent returned 5 candidate findings; all 5 rejected after validation: (1) provider atom normalization already fixed in 15d3216, (2) Runner ToolCallDone re-parsing from buffer is correct streaming pattern, (3) research runner crash handling works via async_nolink's built-in monitor, (4) nil usage in RateLimiter.reconcile is unreachable with current Anthropic provider, (5) research_model nil impossible because Config struct defaults job_lead_model to claude-sonnet-4. All prior work list items completed per specd_history.md.

summary: Fixed Runner.reconcile provider key mismatch
reasoning: Changed runner.ex:160 to use :provider_name instead of :provider when calling RateLimiter.reconcile. The runner_config passed to Runner.run contains both :provider (module atom like Deft.Provider.Anthropic) and :provider_name (string like 'anthropic'). RateLimiter uses strings as bucket keys, so both request (line 232) and reconcile (line 160) must use :provider_name. The mismatch was causing tokens to be debited on request but never credited back on reconcile.

summary: Confirmed finding: Runner.reconcile uses wrong config key, passing module atom instead of string to RateLimiter
reasoning: runner.ex:160 uses Map.get(config, :provider, 'anthropic') which returns the module atom Deft.Provider.Anthropic from runner_config, while request_llm_call at line 232 correctly uses :provider_name key to get the string 'anthropic'. RateLimiter debits string key but reconcile credits atom key — mismatched bucket keys mean tokens never get credited back.

summary: Rejected finding: research task crash handling uses wrong ref type
reasoning: Agent claimed Process.monitor creates a ref that doesn't match task.ref in crash handler. Rejected because Task.Supervisor.async_nolink already monitors internally and sends DOWN with task.ref on crash. The explicit Process.monitor is redundant but harmless — the async_nolink DOWN message correctly matches task.ref in the lookup.

summary: Added work_cost_ceiling to CLI agent_config map
reasoning: Fixed KeyError in Job.Supervisor.init/1 by adding work_cost_ceiling: config.work_cost_ceiling to the agent_config map built in cli.ex:2058-2078. This ensures the supervisor can access config.work_cost_ceiling at line 83 when starting the RateLimiter. Chose to add the key to the config map rather than using Map.get in the supervisor for consistency with other config fields.

summary: Normalized provider config from atom to string in get_provider/1
reasoning: The CLI sets provider as module atom (Deft.Provider.Anthropic) but Registry.resolve/2 expects string names ('anthropic'). Added normalize_provider_name/1 helper that converts module atoms to lowercase string names (Deft.Provider.Anthropic -> 'anthropic') while passing through existing strings. Applied to both Foreman and Lead get_provider functions to prevent FunctionClauseError at first LLM call.

summary: Rejected finding: post-merge test task not monitored
reasoning: Agent claimed Process.monitor is never called after async_nolink for post-merge test tasks. Rejected because Task.Supervisor.async_nolink/2 creates a monitor internally — task.ref IS the monitor reference. DOWN messages will arrive on crash. The code correctly stores task.ref as the lookup key.

summary: Rejected finding: analyze_verification_results misclassifies Runner errors as test failures
reasoning: Agent claimed {:error, reason} from Runner crash falls to default '_ -> false' branch, deleting the job branch on transient failures. While technically accurate, the spec does not prescribe different handling for infra errors vs test failures. This is a code quality concern, not a spec violation. The keep_branch config flag mitigates data loss.

summary: Confirmed finding: config.work_cost_ceiling KeyError crashes Job.Supervisor.init/1
reasoning: supervisor.ex:83 accesses config.work_cost_ceiling via dot accessor on a plain map. The CLI (cli.ex:2058-2078) builds agent_config without a work_cost_ceiling key. Elixir's dot accessor on a plain map raises KeyError for missing keys. The job supervisor cannot start. Prior history item (orchestration v0.3) added the config.work_cost_ceiling line but the CLI was never updated to include the key.

summary: Confirmed finding: get_provider/1 passes atom to Registry.resolve which requires binary
reasoning: CLI sets provider: Deft.Provider.Anthropic (atom) in agent_config. Foreman get_provider (foreman.ex:2409) and Lead get_provider (lead.ex:830) retrieve this atom via Map.get and pass it to Provider.Registry.resolve/2 which has is_binary guard (registry.ex:59). FunctionClauseError crashes the process at first LLM call. Prior history item fixed the Runner→RateLimiter path but not this Foreman/Lead→Registry path.

summary: Implemented send_user_message/2 to deliver job results to user via session and CLI
reasoning: Replaced the stub with a full implementation that: (1) creates an assistant Message with the text content, (2) appends to the Foreman's messages list, (3) persists via save_unsaved_messages, (4) sends {:job_message, message} to cli_pid for immediate display during job execution. Updated both call sites (verification failure at line 1199, merge failure at line 1154) to capture returned data. Added handler in CLI wait_for_job_completion to display job messages. This ensures users see verification failures and merge errors both immediately during execution and in the persistent session history.

summary: Made post-merge tests asynchronous in Foreman to prevent 5-minute blocking
reasoning: Changed handle_successful_merge to spawn async Task.Supervisor.async_nolink task instead of synchronous GitJob.run_post_merge_tests call. Added post_merge_test_tasks map to track running tests per lead_id. Added handler for task completion (success, test_failed, error) and DOWN message handler for crashes. Foreman now remains responsive to Lead messages, DOWN signals, and user input during post-merge tests. Follows the same pattern as merge_resolution_tasks for per-lead async work.

summary: Rejected finding: RateLimiter crashes when usage is nil from Runner stream
reasoning: collect_stream_events initializes usage=nil and could pass nil to RateLimiter.reconcile where Map.get(nil, :input, ...) would crash. However, the only implemented provider (Anthropic) always sends %Usage{} before %Done{}. No realistic crash scenario exists with current code. Defensive fix is low priority.

summary: Confirmed finding: send_user_message is a no-op stub — job results never reach the user
reasoning: send_user_message at foreman.ex:3217 only logs. Called at line 1094 (merge failure after verification) and line 1139 (verification failure). The user never learns whether the job succeeded or failed. Spec section 7 requires user interaction during jobs.

summary: Confirmed finding: Foreman blocks up to 5 min during post-merge tests
reasoning: process_lead_message(:complete, ...) at foreman.ex:1802 synchronously calls run_post_merge_tests which uses Task.yield(task, 300_000) in git/job.ex:543. This blocks the gen_statem handle_event callback for up to 5 minutes, making the Foreman unresponsive to all Lead messages, DOWN signals, and user input. Spec section 1 states handle_event callback mode is 'critical for the Foreman to handle incoming Lead messages while in any agent state'.

summary: Added job-level max_duration timeout enforcement in Foreman
reasoning: Implemented by setting a timer in init that sends :job_timeout message after job_max_duration (default 1_800_000ms). Handler aborts job by killing all Leads and tasks, cleaning up worktrees, archiving files, and transitioning to complete. Timer is cancelled in all normal and error completion paths.

summary: Added verification Runner timeout using job_runner_timeout config
reasoning: Following the existing pattern from research_timeout, added timeout enforcement for verification Runners to prevent jobs from hanging indefinitely during verification phase. Used Process.send_after with timeout handler that kills the task and fails the job with cleanup.

summary: Confirmed finding: job.max_duration not enforced — no job-level timeout
reasoning: Spec section 8 lists job.max_duration (default 1_800_000ms) as a config field. The config key was added to Deft.Config but the Foreman never reads it or sets a timer. Without it, a stuck job can run forever with no recovery.

summary: Confirmed finding: Verification Runner has no timeout — job hangs if runner stalls
reasoning: Foreman start_verification (foreman.ex:504-561) spawns a verification Runner but sets no timeout timer. Research phase uses job_research_timeout with Process.send_after (line 340), but verification has no equivalent. If the verification Runner hangs (not crashes — hangs), the job stays in {:verifying, :idle} indefinitely.

summary: Rejected agent finding: Verification Runner crash causes permanent hang due to missing monitor
reasoning: Same async_nolink reasoning — the Task module already sets up monitoring. The verification Runner crash IS handled by the existing DOWN handler at foreman.ex:729 via tool_tasks matching. No additional Process.monitor needed.

summary: Rejected agent finding: Task ref vs monitor ref mismatch in research DOWN handler
reasoning: Agent claimed task.ref != monitor_ref in DOWN messages, but Task.Supervisor.async_nolink uses the same ref for both the Task struct and the monitor. The DOWN message from async_nolink will have ref == task.ref. The crash detection at foreman.ex:696 works correctly. The redundant Process.monitor at line 335 creates a harmless second DOWN that falls through as a no-op.

summary: Added DOWN handler for research runner crashes in Foreman
reasoning: Modified the DOWN message handler to check both research_tasks and tool_tasks. When a research runner crashes, Process.monitor sends a DOWN message but the old handler only checked tool_tasks, causing crashed research runners to remain in research_tasks and hang the phase. Now uses cond to first check research_tasks, then tool_tasks, then falls through to Lead crash handler. When all research tasks are done (including crashed ones), transitions to decomposing with whatever findings were collected.

summary: Lead verification phase now spawns testing Runner instead of self-prompting
reasoning: Lead only has read-only tools [Read, Grep, Find, Ls] and cannot run bash commands. Spec v0.4 section 4.2 explicitly requires spawning a testing Runner with bash access for compile checks and tests. Changed handle_event for {:verifying, :idle} to call spawn_runner with :testing runner type. Added build_runner_context_for_verification/1 helper.

summary: Fixed Lead spawn_runner to build proper runner_config with provider module
reasoning: Lead.spawn_runner was passing data.config directly to Runner.run, but data.config[:provider] is a string like 'anthropic'. Runner expects config[:provider] to be a module it can call provider.stream() on. Following the pattern from Foreman (foreman.ex:316-320), built a proper runner_config map with provider: get_provider(data) (module), provider_name: provider_name (string), and model: runner_model. This matches the Foreman's approach and fixes the UndefinedFunctionError at runtime.

summary: Confirmed: Research runner crashes hang research phase — no DOWN handler for research_tasks
reasoning: Process.monitor(task.pid) at foreman.ex:335 creates a monitor ref distinct from task.ref. The DOWN handler at foreman.ex:684 checks tool_tasks (not research_tasks) with task.ref == ref. Crashed research runners are never removed from research_tasks, so Enum.empty?(tasks) at line 822 never becomes true. Phase hangs until 120s timeout.

summary: Confirmed: Lead verification phase prompts itself instead of spawning a testing Runner
reasoning: lead.ex:235-242 enters {:verifying, :idle} and sends {:prompt, verification_prompt} to itself. The verification prompt tells the LLM to run compile checks and tests, but the Lead's tools are read-only [Read, Grep, Find, Ls]. Spec v0.4 explicitly clarified that verification requires spawning a testing Runner.

summary: Confirmed: Lead spawn_runner passes data.config with string provider to Runner expecting module
reasoning: lead.ex:1083 passes data.config to Runner.run. data.config[:provider] is a string like 'anthropic' (see lead.ex:817). Runner.run at runner.ex:97 reads config[:provider] and calls provider.stream() at runner.ex:244, crashing with UndefinedFunctionError. Compare with Foreman's research runner (foreman.ex:316-317) which correctly builds runner_config with provider: get_provider(data).

summary: Fixed Foreman phase transition to always use :idle agent_state
reasoning: The :verifying phase enter handler (foreman.ex:387) and :start_verification cast handler (foreman.ex:505) only match {:verifying, :idle}. When the last Lead completes while Foreman is in :calling or :streaming state and transitions to :verifying, it would preserve the agent_state causing verification to never start. Changed line 653 to transition to {new_phase, :idle} instead of {new_phase, agent_state}, and prefixed agent_state parameter with underscore since it's now unused.

summary: Fixed Lead execute_tool to return {tool_call.id, result} tuple
reasoning: Changed execute_tool/2 to return {tool_call.id, result} instead of bare result. This matches the expected format for build_tool_result_blocks/2 which pattern-matches {tool_use_id, tool_result}. Previously :ok or :error atoms became tool_use_id values, causing malformed ToolResult blocks that the Anthropic API rejected.

summary: Fixed Foreman and Lead call_llm to use configured provider and role-specific model
reasoning: The hardcoded Anthropic.stream call ignored user-configured provider settings and used config.model (session model) instead of job_foreman_model/job_lead_model. Now both modules use get_provider to resolve the configured provider module and pass the role-specific model via llm_config

summary: Confirmed: Foreman lead message handler preserves agent_state on phase transition, causing verification hang
reasoning: foreman.ex:653 transitions to {new_phase, agent_state} but :verifying enter handler only matches {:verifying, :idle}. If last Lead completes while Foreman is streaming, verification never starts. Agent reported this; validated by reading foreman.ex:640-658 and 388-392.

summary: Confirmed: Lead execute_tool returns bare {:ok, content} instead of {tool_use_id, result} tuple
reasoning: Lead execute_tool (lead.ex:726-747) returns {:ok, content} from tool_module.execute, but build_tool_result_blocks (lead.ex:1004) pattern-matches {tool_use_id, tool_result}. The :ok atom becomes tool_use_id, producing malformed ToolResult blocks. Distinct from known placeholder issue — this is a type mismatch in the real implementation.

summary: Confirmed: Foreman and Lead call_llm hardcode Anthropic.stream instead of using resolved provider
reasoning: foreman.ex:1145 and lead.ex:786 call Anthropic.stream directly. get_provider/1 exists and correctly resolves the provider from config but is only used for Runner configs. Non-Anthropic provider config is silently ignored. Also job_foreman_model/job_lead_model config keys are not passed — session model is used instead.

summary: Rejected: Foreman empty tools list is intentional design, not a bug
reasoning: Agent reported foreman.ex:1141 tools=[] as a finding. The code comment explicitly says 'Foreman uses empty tools list - it delegates actual work to Runners'. Spec section 3.1 confirms research is done by Runners. The Foreman plans by reasoning about prompts, not by reading files directly. The executing_tools state is inherited from Agent base but intentionally unused.

summary: Fixed execute_tools_inline to return single Message with all ToolResult blocks
reasoning: Changed execute_tools_inline to collect all ToolResult content blocks into a single list and return one %Message{role: :user, content: tool_result_blocks} instead of creating separate messages per tool result. Updated the call site (line 191-192) to handle a single message instead of spreading a list. This fixes the Anthropic API alternating-role violation that occurred on multi-tool turns, where consecutive :user messages caused API errors.

summary: Normalized Runner rate limiter provider key to use string instead of module atom
reasoning: Runner was passing module atom (Deft.Provider.Anthropic) while Foreman/Lead passed string ('anthropic'), creating separate rate limiter buckets. Added :provider_name key to runner_config with string value, updated Runner to use it for RateLimiter.request calls. This ensures all three components use the same provider key for centralized rate limiting.

summary: Added failure marker tracking to resolve Lead crash/start failure hang
reasoning: Fixed the hang issue where crashed or failed-to-start Leads never write :complete markers, causing all_leads_complete? to wait forever. Solution: (1) Added write_failure_marker helper that writes :failed entries to site log with deliverable name and reason. (2) Added determine_failed_deliverables helper that reads failed-* entries from site log. (3) Updated all_leads_complete? to check finished_count (completed + failed) == deliverables_count instead of only completed_count. (4) Updated all failure paths to write failure markers: handle_lead_crash, handle_test_failure, handle_test_error, handle_merge_error, and both Lead start failure cases. (5) Added :failed to stable key categories in generate_stable_or_unique_key. This allows jobs to complete even when some deliverables fail, while preserving the fc68dda fix that prevents false completion on incomplete work.

summary: Moved completion marker write from process_lead_message to handle_test_success
reasoning: Fixed bug where completion marker was written before merge+test, causing all_leads_complete? to return true even when merge/tests failed. The marker is now written in handle_test_success (after tests pass), preventing false transitions to :verifying with unmerged code. Extracted deliverable name from lead_id format (session_id-deliverable_name) to construct proper metadata.

summary: Audited Lead code to confirm section 4.2 compliance
reasoning: Verified that Lead's execute_tool (line 731) and call_llm (line 783) both use read-only tool set [Read, Grep, Find, Ls]. Lead has no bash access and must delegate all execution/testing to Runners. Updated moduledoc comment (line 29) to clarify that 'runs compile checks' means 'spawns testing Runners to verify compile checks' - matching v0.4 spec clarification.

summary: Updated spec section 4.2 to clarify that Lead spawns testing Runners for compile checks — not executing bash directly
reasoning: Review finding identified ambiguous wording in section 4.2: 'runs compile checks' could imply the Lead has direct bash access. Clarified to specify that the Lead delegates compile checks to testing Runners, maintaining the design where Leads are managers, not executors. This matches the actual code implementation where Lead's tool set is read-only [Read, Grep, Find, Ls].

summary: Promoted review finding to work item: audit Lead code to confirm section 4.2 implementation
reasoning: Review finding identified a mismatch between spec wording ('runs compile checks') and implementation understanding. While the code is correct (Lead uses read-only tools), creating an audit work item ensures the implementation, comments, and spec remain aligned. This prevents future confusion about Lead/Runner responsibilities.

summary: Confirmed: completion marker written before merge causes false transition to :verifying
reasoning: process_lead_message(:complete, ...) writes completion marker to site log at foreman.ex:1619 BEFORE calling handle_lead_merge at line 1628. If merge fails (handle_merge_error), the marker persists. all_leads_complete? sees the marker + empty leads map and returns true, transitioning to :verifying with unmerged code.

summary: Confirmed: fc68dda fix created regression — Lead crash/start failure permanently hangs job
reasoning: fc68dda added completed_count == deliverables_count check to all_leads_complete?. Crashed or failed-to-start Leads never write :complete markers. So completed_count < deliverables_count forever, and the job hangs with no recovery. The original fix was correct for preventing false positives but needs a recovery mechanism for failed deliverables.

summary: Confirmed: Runner rate limiter uses module atom vs Lead/Foreman string — separate buckets
reasoning: Runner config has provider: get_provider(data) which returns a module atom (Deft.Provider.Anthropic). Lead/Foreman config has provider: "anthropic" (string from Config struct). Both call Map.get(config, :provider, "anthropic") but get different values. RateLimiter.ensure_provider creates separate bucket sets per key. The previous fix (2e83342) corrected the key name (:provider_name → :provider) but not the value type mismatch.

summary: Confirmed: Runner execute_tools_inline returns separate messages per tool result — API error on multi-tool turns
reasoning: execute_tools_inline (runner.ex:357-446) returns a list of %Message{role: :user} structs, one per tool result. handle_assistant_message spreads them into the conversation at line 192, creating consecutive :user messages. Anthropic API rejects consecutive same-role messages. Multi-tool assistant turns will always fail.

summary: Sent to review: Lead read-only tools vs spec compile check requirement
reasoning: Lead's tool set is hardcoded to [Read, Grep, Find, Ls] — no bash. Spec section 4.2 says Lead 'runs compile checks after each Runner'. Ambiguous whether this means the Lead runs them directly (needs bash) or spawns a testing Runner. Sent to specd_review.md for human decision rather than filing a work item.

summary: Rejected agent finding: Runner stream events not arriving
reasoning: Agent suggested Anthropic.stream events might not reach the Runner process. Rejected because Anthropic.stream sends events to the calling process (self()), and the Runner's collect_loop receives from its own mailbox. Both Lead and Runner use the same pattern — stream sends to caller. No evidence of breakage.

summary: Rejected agent finding: plan atom/string key mismatch on resume
reasoning: Agent suggested plan resume path might use string keys vs atom keys. Verified: parse_plan_from_json, extract_plan_from_messages, and parse_json_plan all consistently use atom keys (:deliverables, :dependencies, etc.). all_leads_complete? uses atom key Map.get(data.plan, :deliverables). No mismatch.

summary: Fixed Lead crash falsely satisfying all_leads_complete? by checking site log
reasoning: When a Lead crashes, handle_lead_crash removes it from data.leads but leaves it in data.started_leads. The old all_leads_complete? only checked that started_count == deliverables_count and remaining_leads == 0, which could be satisfied even when a crashed Lead's deliverable was incomplete. Fixed by adding a check that all started deliverables have a corresponding completion record in the site log via determine_completed_deliverables. This ensures crashed Leads (which never write a :complete marker to the site log) cannot falsely trigger completion.

summary: Fixed provider_name key mismatch in Foreman, Lead, and Runner call_llm
reasoning: Changed Map.get(config, :provider_name, "anthropic") to Map.get(config, :provider, "anthropic") in 4 locations: foreman.ex:1127, lead.ex:776, runner.ex:160, runner.ex:232. The Config struct has :provider field, not :provider_name. The original code always fell back to "anthropic" default, ignoring user-configured provider. While the work item only mentioned foreman.ex and lead.ex, I also fixed runner.ex which had the same issue to ensure consistency.

summary: Fixed Runner provider guard to halt on nil provider
reasoning: Replaced the non-functional 'unless provider' guard (lines 99-101) with 'if is_nil(provider), do: raise ArgumentError' to immediately halt execution when provider is nil. The old guard created an error tuple but discarded it, allowing execution to continue with a nil provider and crash later at provider.stream/3 with a misleading UndefinedFunctionError. The new approach raises an explicit configuration error at the right point.

summary: Marked issues v0.4 as Implemented — no findings
reasoning: Audit found no functional bugs in the issues implementation. The research agent's only finding (cycle detection misses cycles via already-visited nodes) was rejected: DFS from any cycle member follows all outgoing edges, so cycles are always detected during the first member's traversal. The visited-set optimization only skips nodes whose full subtree was already explored.

summary: Filed 4 orchestration findings: config key mismatches, non-functional guard, false completion on Lead crash
reasoning: Four confirmed findings: (1) Lead reads :runner_timeout/:max_runners_per_lead but Config struct has :job_runner_timeout/:job_max_runners_per_lead — user config silently ignored. (2) Runner unless-provider guard discards return value, nil provider crashes with misleading error. (3) Crashed Lead removed from data.leads but stays in started_leads, falsely satisfying all_leads_complete? — Foreman transitions to :verifying with incomplete work. (4) Foreman/Lead call_llm reads :provider_name but Config has :provider — always defaults to 'anthropic'. Finding 5 from research agent (start_lead failure) was rejected — that behavior is the intentional fix from a prior implementation.

summary: Wire CLI plan approval flow via message protocol
reasoning: Added a message-based protocol where Foreman sends {:plan_approval_needed, plan} to the CLI process when waiting for plan approval in {:decomposing, :idle} state. The CLI displays the plan and prompts the user (y/n), then calls Foreman.approve_plan/1 or Foreman.reject_plan/1. This enables the approve-every-plan workflow from the spec without the CLI hanging indefinitely. Used direct message passing instead of a registered name to avoid potential race conditions during process registration.

summary: Add deliverable to started_leads on Lead start failure
reasoning: When LeadSupervisor.start_lead or worktree creation fails, the deliverable must still be added to started_leads to satisfy all_leads_complete? which checks started_count == deliverables_count. Without this, a failed Lead start causes the job to hang forever in :executing since the counts never match. Also added worktree cleanup when LeadSupervisor.start_lead fails since the worktree was already created. This is simpler than changing all_leads_complete? logic and correctly reflects that we attempted to start all deliverables even if some failed immediately.

summary: Added phase transition check after Lead crash in Foreman DOWN handler
reasoning: When a Lead crashes in the :executing phase, the Foreman removes it from the leads map but must check if all leads are now complete. Without this check, if the last Lead crashes, the job hangs in :executing forever instead of transitioning to :verifying. Used the existing check_phase_transition pattern from merge completion handler.

summary: Added DOWN handler for tool task crashes including verification Runner
reasoning: When a verification Runner (or other tool task) crashes, it sends a {:DOWN, ref, :process, pid, reason} message. The previous code only had handlers for stream crashes and Lead crashes - tool task crashes would fall through to the Lead handler, not find a matching Lead, return :keep_state_and_data, and cause the job to hang forever. Added a new handler before the Lead crash handler that checks if the ref matches any task in data.tool_tasks. For verification Runner crashes (in :verifying phase), it logs the error, cleans up worktrees, archives job files, and transitions to {:complete, :idle}. For other phases, it removes the crashed task and keeps state. Extracted Lead crash logic to handle_lead_crash/3 helper and moved it to the private helpers section after all handle_event clauses to avoid Elixir clause grouping warnings.

decision: Added stream process DOWN handlers in both Lead and Foreman to prevent hangs when stream processes crash
rationale: When a stream process crashes, the DOWN message was being silently dropped by the catch-all handler in Lead (lead.ex:542-548) and the nil-case in Foreman's Lead crash handler (foreman.ex:666-668). Added specific handlers that check for monitor_ref == data.stream_monitor_ref and reset streaming state (stream_ref, stream_monitor_ref, current_message to nil), then transition to {current_phase, :idle}. Lead also sends an error message to Foreman. Guards ensure the handler only fires when agent_state is :calling or :streaming.

summary: Added contract message sending logic to Lead when runner completes interface work
reasoning: Implemented heuristic-based detection of interface contracts using keyword matching in task descriptions and runner output. When detected, Lead sends :contract messages to Foreman to enable partial dependency unblocking per spec §3.3 and §4.3

summary: Implemented real tool execution in Lead using pattern from Runner
reasoning: Lead's execute_tool/2 was returning placeholder results. Implemented real execution by: 1) building tool_context from Lead's data (worktree_path, session_id, lead_id, cache_tid), 2) creating tool map from available tools [Read, Grep, Find, Ls], 3) looking up tool module by name, 4) calling tool_module.execute(args, context) with error handling. Pattern matches Runner's execute_tools_inline approach but adapted for Lead's context.

summary: Marked git-strategy v0.1 as Implemented — no findings
reasoning: Audited all 3 agent findings: (1) complete_job ordering — Lead worktrees are cleaned up in handle_test_success before verification, so verify_no_worktrees passes; (2) merge-resolution retry — after resolution commit, re-merge is trivial 'Already up to date'; (3) orphan worktree scan — spec only requires deft/lead-* worktrees and deft/job-* branches, both covered. All findings rejected.

summary: Filed 2 work items for issues v0.4: normalize_status fallback and CLI plan approval flow
reasoning: (1) normalize_status/normalize_source have no fallback clause — unrecognized values from bad merges crash Issues GenServer init via FunctionClauseError; load_issues only catches {:error, reason} from Jason.decode, not exceptions from from_map. (2) CLI wait_for_job_completion has no plan-approval message handling — Foreman has server-side approval gate (present_plan_for_approval + approve_plan handler) but CLI never sends approval, causing hang in decomposing:idle without --auto-approve-all.

summary: Filed 6 work items for orchestration v0.3: Lead execute_tool placeholder, missing contract messages, stream DOWN, verification crash, Lead crash transition, start failure hang
reasoning: Rejected agent findings: (1) phase transition after normal completion — works correctly via check_phase_transition in lead_message handler; (2) Foreman execute_tool placeholder — Foreman passes tools=[] so execute_tool is dead code; (3) Runner missing provider early return — rescue block catches crash. Confirmed 6 findings: Lead execute_tool returns fake results while pipeline is functional; Lead never sends :contract messages; stream DOWN silently dropped; verification Runner crash unhandled; Lead crash doesn't trigger phase check; start_lead failure breaks all_leads_complete?.

summary: Rejected agent finding: deft work --loop crashes after first job
reasoning: Agent claimed run_work_on_issue_with_cost returns :ok bare, but run_work_on_issue at cli.ex:2093-2094 maps handle_job_result's :ok to {:ok, cost}, so the loop pattern match {:ok, job_cost} works correctly.

summary: Derive publishing_deliverable from Foreman's Lead tracking map instead of metadata
reasoning: Lead's send_lead_message/4 never populates :deliverable_name in metadata, so contract_matches? always returned false (guards on publishing_deliverable != nil). Fixed by looking up the deliverable name from data.leads map using lead_id, enabling contract-based dependency unblocking per spec section 3.3.

summary: Changed delete_lead_branch to use git branch -D instead of -d
reasoning: Merge happens in temporary worktree, not main HEAD. From main repo perspective, lead branch is never merged, so -d (safe delete) always fails with 'not fully merged'. -D (force delete) is correct per spec section 6 line 99.

summary: Added commit instruction to merge-resolution Runner
reasoning: The Runner was instructed to stage resolved files but never commit them. When cleanup_worktree removed the merge worktree, staged changes were lost and the merge retry would see the same unresolved conflicts. Updated instructions to commit after staging, ensuring resolved state persists for the merge retry.

summary: Guarded {:executing, :idle} enter handler to skip branch creation on resume
reasoning: When resuming a job, the state machine transitions to {:executing, :idle} but the job branch already exists. The enter handler was unconditionally calling create_job_branch, which failed and caused immediate transition to {:complete, :idle}, aborting the resumed job. Added check for data.config.resume to skip branch creation when resuming, since the branch was created during the original job start.

summary: Added phase transition check after merge-resolution completion
reasoning: The merge-resolution handler at foreman.ex:831-832 was returning {:keep_state, new_data} without checking if all Leads are complete. If the last Lead required conflict resolution, the job would permanently stall in :executing and never enter :verifying. Added call to check_phase_transition(:complete, :executing, new_data) after successful merge retry, matching the pattern used in the lead message handler at line 637.

summary: Auto-start Issues from Application when issues.jsonl exists
reasoning: Implemented conditional auto-start in Application.start/2 using file existence check and application config. Added :auto_start_issues config set to false in test environment to allow tests to start Issues with specific file paths. Used Application.get_env instead of Mix.env to avoid Credo warning about Mix calls in application code.

summary: Changed SIGINT timeout rollback message to explicit warning
reasoning: Spec v0.4 §5.3 requires emitting a warning when manually rolling back issue status after SIGINT timeout. Changed the success message from stdout info to stderr warning to emphasize that manual intervention was needed.

summary: Updated issues spec to v0.4 documenting actual SIGINT timeout behavior
reasoning: Review confirmed code's safer behavior (manual rollback to :open on timeout) vs spec's stale-detection model (leave at :in_progress). Updated spec v0.4 to document that CLI manually rolls back with a warning to prevent orphaned in-progress issues.

summary: Promoted SIGINT timeout review finding to work item
reasoning: Review approved code's safe behavior. Created work item to add warning message to cli.ex when timeout expires, matching spec v0.4 requirements.

summary: SIGINT timeout rollback behavior sent to review
reasoning: Spec says leave issue at :in_progress on timeout; code rolls back to :open. Code behavior is arguably safer. Sent to specd_review.md for human decision.

summary: IssueCreate uses File.cwd!() instead of context working_dir
reasoning: issue_create.ex:175 loads config from OS cwd instead of the session's working_dir from Context. Wrong compaction_days applied when --working-dir differs.

summary: Issues not started by Deft.Application on startup
reasoning: Spec §4 requires startup by Application if .deft/issues.jsonl exists. Currently only started ad-hoc. Missing startup path.

summary: Rejected: keys: :atoms crash on unknown fields
reasoning: Agent claimed Jason.decode with keys: :atoms would crash on unknown field names. Actually, keys: :atoms uses String.to_atom/1 (not to_existing_atom/1), so it creates new atoms without crashing. Not a functional bug.

summary: delete_lead_branch uses -d (safe delete) which always fails
reasoning: Merge happens in temporary worktree, not main HEAD. git branch -d requires branch merged into HEAD, which it isn't. -d always fails; return value discarded by callers. Branches silently accumulate.

summary: Merge-resolution retry discards resolved state
reasoning: Runner stages resolved files but never commits. Worktree cleanup at foreman.ex:825 removes staged files. Retry at line 831 creates new merge with same conflicts. Resolution permanently lost.

summary: Resume path re-creates job branch causing immediate failure
reasoning: Entering {:executing, :idle} always calls create_job_branch. Resume enters this state but branch already exists. create_branch fails, enter handler transitions to {:complete, :idle}, aborting the job.

summary: Rejected: post-merge test failure not spawning fix-up Runner
reasoning: Spec says 'spawns a fix-up Runner OR flags for user intervention'. Code flags for user intervention. This is a valid implementation of the spec's OR clause.

summary: Contract matching uses metadata :deliverable_name that Lead never provides
reasoning: foreman.ex:1344 reads deliverable_name from metadata. Lead's send_lead_message never populates this. contract_matches? guards publishing_deliverable != nil, so always returns false. Foreman should derive from its own Lead tracking map.

summary: Merge-resolution completion skips phase transition check
reasoning: foreman.ex:831-832 returns {:keep_state, new_data} after merge-resolution without calling check_phase_transition. If last Lead needed conflict resolution, job permanently stalls in :executing.

summary: RateLimiter ignores configured work_cost_ceiling
reasoning: supervisor.ex:79 starts RateLimiter with only job_id and foreman_pid. rate_limiter.ex:336 defaults cost_ceiling to 10.0 but config.ex:163 has work.cost_ceiling: 50.0. User-configured ceiling never reaches RateLimiter.

summary: Pass compaction_days config to Issues.start_link in IssueCreate tool
reasoning: The IssueCreate.ensure_issues_started/0 function was calling Issues.start_link() with no arguments, causing the Issues GenServer to always use the default 90-day compaction period regardless of user's issues.compaction_days config. Fixed by loading config and passing compaction_days: config.issues_compaction_days, matching the pattern used in cli.ex:86. Also added Deft.Config alias per Credo suggestion.

summary: Fixed Foreman config keys to use job_ prefix as specified
reasoning: The orchestration spec defines config keys with job. prefix (e.g. job.max_leads). These are stored in Deft.Config struct as flattened keys with job_ prefix. The production code in cli.ex already uses the flattened format. Updated foreman.ex to read :job_max_leads, :job_lead_model, :job_runner_model, :job_research_timeout, :job_research_runner_model instead of unprefixed versions. Also updated test fixtures to match.

summary: Added tool_results: [] to Lead initial_data map
reasoning: The add_tool_results/2 function at lead.ex:898 uses map update syntax %{data | tool_results: new_accumulated} which requires the key to exist. Without this key in initial_data, Lead crashes with KeyError on first tool execution.

summary: IssueCreate tool starts Issues GenServer without compaction_days config
reasoning: issue_create.ex:173 calls Issues.start_link() with no arguments. The CLI path was fixed previously but the tool path was missed. User-configured issues.compaction_days is ignored when the tool starts the GenServer first.

summary: Rejected: deft work <id> missing blocker check is not a finding
reasoning: The spec says 'Run a specific issue as a job' without requiring blocker checking for explicit ID selection. Only deft work (no args) uses the ready queue which excludes blocked issues. Direct ID selection is intentionally unrestricted.

summary: Original branch never captured — always squash-merges into main
reasoning: foreman.ex:900 uses Map.get(data.config, :original_branch, 'main') but :original_branch is not in Config struct and is never stored anywhere. The branch the user was on at job start is lost.

summary: Rejected: config field name mismatch belongs to orchestration spec, not git-strategy
reasoning: The mismatched config keys (max_leads, lead_model, runner_model, etc.) are orchestration config fields defined in orchestration spec section 8. Git-strategy config fields (job_test_command, job_keep_failed_branches, job_squash_on_complete) are accessed correctly.

summary: Foreman uses wrong config key names — missing job_ prefix
reasoning: Config struct has :job_max_leads, :job_lead_model, etc. but Foreman uses :max_leads, :lead_model, etc. Map.get on struct for non-existent key returns nil, so defaults always used. User config silently ignored.

summary: Lead missing tool_results in initial_data — KeyError on first tool execution
reasoning: lead.ex:97-124 initial_data map omits tool_results key. add_tool_results/2 at line 898 uses map update syntax which requires key to exist. Foreman has tool_results: [] but Lead does not.

summary: Rejected: Runner streaming collect_loop is valid — not a bug
reasoning: Runner accumulates ToolCallDelta events into buffer.args_json and parses on ToolCallDone. This is the standard streaming pattern. ToolCallDone.args exists but Runner's approach of building from deltas works correctly.

summary: Rejected: verification handler matching any ref is too theoretical
reasoning: The {ref, results} match in :verifying state could theoretically consume stale task messages, but the state machine processes previous tasks before transitioning. A message leaking across state transitions is extremely unlikely in practice.

summary: add_tool_results/2 in Lead was already implemented in b163a44
reasoning: Work item was left in specd_work_list.md after being implemented. The function was added as supporting code when implementing finalize_streaming/1 in commit b163a44. Verified implementations in Foreman and Lead are identical.

summary: Implemented add_tool_results/2 in Foreman to inject tool results into message list
reasoning: The placeholder implementation was a no-op preventing Foreman agent loop from completing tool execution cycles. Changed execute_tool/2 to return {tool_use_id, result} tuple for proper mapping. Added tool_results field to data for accumulation. Implemented add_tool_results/2 to accumulate results and finalize_tool_results/2 to build user message when all tasks complete. Extracted build_tool_result_blocks/2 and build_tool_result_block/3 helpers to reduce nesting depth per Credo requirements.

summary: Implemented finalize_streaming/1 in Foreman to build complete assistant message from stream
reasoning: The placeholder implementation only called save_unsaved_messages/1 which had nothing to save because streaming events accumulated in data.current_message were never moved to data.messages. Now checks if current_message exists, appends it to messages list, resets current_message to nil, and calls save_unsaved_messages to persist the complete message to the session file.

summary: Added orchestration config keys to Deft.Config and wired through CLI agent_config
reasoning: Per spec section 8, orchestration needs 9 new config fields (job_max_leads, job_max_runners_per_lead, job_research_timeout, job_runner_timeout, job_foreman_model, job_lead_model, job_runner_model, job_research_runner_model, job_max_duration). Added to Config struct with defaults, parsed from YAML under job.* namespace, and wired through CLI's agent_config map so Foreman/Lead can access them at runtime.

summary: Implemented process_provider_event/2 in Foreman to accumulate streaming events
reasoning: The placeholder implementation discarded all LLM response events. Added pattern matching for all provider event types (TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolCallStart, ToolCallDelta, ToolCallDone, Usage, Done, Error) and implemented accumulation into data.current_message. Reused helper functions from Agent.ex pattern: append_text_delta/2, append_thinking_delta/2, update_tool_call_args/3. Removed duplicate generate_message_id/0 as it already existed at line 1722.

summary: Fixed merge_lead_branch/1 @spec to match actual 4-tuple return type on conflict
reasoning: The @spec declared conflict case as {:ok, :conflict, [String.t()]} but implementation returns {:ok, :conflict, [String.t()], String.t()} including temp_dir path. Callers written to the published spec would crash on conflict. Updated spec to include fourth element (temp_dir) as String.t().

summary: Fixed run_work_on_issue to use Job.Supervisor instead of direct Foreman.start_link
reasoning: The direct Foreman.start_link call was missing the required :runner_supervisor argument, causing KeyError at foreman.ex:66. Job.Supervisor properly sets up the full supervision tree including RateLimiter, Store, RunnerSupervisor, and Foreman with all required arguments. Extracted helper functions get_foreman_pid/1 and get_job_cost/1 to reduce cyclomatic complexity.

summary: Fixed Foreman.resume/1 to pass runner_supervisor to start_link/1
reasoning: resume/1 was calling start_link/1 without the required :runner_supervisor option, causing KeyError at line 66. Added extraction of runner_supervisor from opts and passed it through to start_link/1. Also updated documentation to reflect this required parameter.

summary: Confirmed: resume/1 missing runner_supervisor crashes on every resume
reasoning: Foreman.resume/1 at foreman.ex:176-183 calls start_link without :runner_supervisor. start_link at line 66 uses Keyword.fetch! which raises KeyError. Verified against code — not in specd_history.md.

summary: Confirmed: Foreman and Lead process_provider_event/finalize_streaming/add_tool_results are no-ops
reasoning: Both foreman.ex:1038-1062 and lead.ex:723-746 have placeholder implementations that discard all streaming events. LLM calls complete but responses are never accumulated into messages. Planning, decomposition, steering, and task management produce no output. Not in specd_history.md.

summary: Confirmed: orchestration config keys missing from Deft.Config
reasoning: Spec section 8 defines job.max_leads, job.max_runners_per_lead, job.research_timeout, job.runner_timeout, job.foreman_model, job.lead_model, job.runner_model, job.research_runner_model, job.max_duration. None exist in Deft.Config struct or defaults. Code uses Map.get with hardcoded defaults. Users cannot configure orchestration parameters.

summary: Rejected: Lead sends deliverable description instead of name in :complete metadata
reasoning: Lead sends data.deliverable (description string) in :complete metadata.
