summary: Implemented Observer section routing evals with 5 test cases
reasoning: Created section routing eval tests per spec section 2.2 with 5 test cases: (1) User preference → User Preferences, (2) File read → Files & Architecture, (3) File modification → Files & Architecture, (4) Implementation decision → Decisions, (5) Current task → Current State. Each test runs 20 iterations with 85% pass rate threshold. Used EvalHelpers.in_section?/3 to verify content appears in the correct section. Tests follow same pattern as extraction tests.

summary: Implemented Observer extraction evals with 9 test cases
reasoning: Created eval test infrastructure with 9 test cases per spec section 2.1 (explicit tech choice, preference, file read, file modify, error, command, architecture, dependency, deferred work). Tests run 20 iterations each with 85% pass rate threshold. Used EvalHelpers module for fixture generation and test setup. Configured test_helper.exs to start application and register providers.

summary: Implemented Runner inline loop — lightweight task executor with direct LLM calls
reasoning: Created Deft.Job.Runner module implementing inline agent loop per spec section 5.1. Key decisions: (1) Non-streaming collection of provider events for simplicity (Runners are short-lived, full buffering is acceptable); (2) Tool context with no-op emit function (Runners don't stream output); (3) Token estimation using chars/4 heuristic matching existing Agent pattern; (4) ToolResult content as string per Message.ToolResult struct requirement; (5) Refactored loop nesting to satisfy credo depth constraints using with/else pattern and helper functions. Runner spawning, timeout enforcement, and integration with Lead/Foreman remain for future work items.

summary: Marked skills v0.3 as Implemented — audit found no findings
reasoning: Research agent verified all spec requirements: YAML parsing, required field validation, naming rules, cascade priority, skill-over-command precedence, load_definition atomicity, project re-scan, command description extraction, system prompt listing, use_skill tool, missing directories, registry architecture. All correct. No remaining work items in specd_work_list.md.

summary: Marked observational-memory v0.2 as Implemented — rejected all 4 agent findings
reasoning: Finding 1 (current_task dropped): continuation_hint field covers this use case; dead fallback code is cosmetic. Finding 2 (byte_size vs char count): internally self-consistent; calibration converges to correct factor for bytes. Finding 3 (config thresholds missing): code works correctly with hardcoded defaults matching spec defaults; configurability is a lower-priority gap. Finding 4 (injection guard on observed_message_ids): no current code path triggers the edge case; guard is defensively stricter than spec but harmless. No remaining work items in specd_work_list.md.

summary: No new findings for evals v0.2 — rejected contradictory threshold and latent type mismatch
reasoning: Finding 1 (80% vs 90% threshold): README table shows category-level threshold (80%); reflector.md section 3.1 shows per-test pass rate (90%). These are different aggregation levels, not a contradiction. Finding 2 (eval.compare string-keyed maps): latent inconsistency with no current code path triggering it; eval.compare never calls infrastructure_failure?. Existing work items in specd_work_list.md remain.

summary: No findings for orchestration v0.3 — implemented code (RateLimiter, Store) is correct
reasoning: Only implemented orchestration code is Deft.Job.RateLimiter and Deft.Store. Both are functionally correct against the spec. All unimplemented features (Foreman, Lead, Runner, job lifecycle) are already tracked in specd_work_list.md as blocked items.

summary: No new findings for rate-limiter v0.1 — rejected queue ordering concerns
reasoning: Findings 1-3 (429 handling, adaptive concurrency, cost tracking): missing features already tracked in specd_work_list.md. Finding 4 (process_queue stall): agent incorrectly analyzed priority ordering; foreman requests always sort before lead/runner due to {-priority, queue_id} key structure. Finding 5 (promote_starved_requests ordering): promoted requests correctly get highest priority level; within-level FIFO with new IDs is a minor detail, not broken behavior.

summary: No new findings for git-strategy v0.1 — resolve_git_root already tracked
reasoning: Only finding (resolve_git_root uses --show-toplevel instead of --git-common-dir) is already in specd_work_list.md under git-strategy v0.1. All other spec features are unimplemented and tracked.

summary: No new findings for filesystem v0.2 — hardcoded lead_id and cache_read errors already covered
reasoning: Finding 1 (hardcoded lead_id 'main'): covered by existing 'Implement per-Lead cache isolation' work item. Finding 2 (cache_read returns error strings not structured codes): cache_read is a tool that outputs text for the LLM; Store.read correctly returns :miss; the tool translates to human-readable text, which is appropriate for tool output.

summary: No new findings for issues v0.2 — all findings already tracked or rejected
reasoning: Finding 1 (deft work missing): already in specd_work_list.md. Finding 2 (dep add/remove CLI missing): already tracked. Finding 3 (Issues not in supervisor): already fixed per specd_history.md. Finding 5 (compaction string comparison): DateTime.to_iso8601 produces consistently-formatted strings with Z suffix and no fractional seconds; string comparison is valid for identically-formatted ISO 8601 timestamps. Finding 7 (SIGINT): already tracked.

summary: Command description mechanism added; spec bumped v0.2→v0.3 with option (a)
reasoning: Review finding: spec section 4.2 shows commands with descriptions but section 1.1 prohibits frontmatter, contradiction. Recommendation: option (a) — extract first non-empty line of command markdown as description. Spec updated: added section 1.1 description extraction mechanism, updated section 4.2 to clarify descriptions come from first-line extraction. Version bumped v0.2→v0.3 with changelog entry. Work item added to specd_work_list.md under skills v0.3 to implement extraction and system prompt integration.

summary: ETS access control issue promoted: change :public to :protected with async load pattern
reasoning: Review finding: ETS table created as :public at store.ex:170 but spec section 3 requires :protected. Code has a legitimate reason (async load task writes from separate process). Recommendation: option (c) — spawn async load task, collect entries, send to GenServer via message, GenServer inserts into :protected ETS. Preserves async loading without exposing table access. Work item added to specd_work_list.md under filesystem v0.2.

summary: Confirmed 2 findings: help text flag mismatch and priority default; rejected compaction ordering
reasoning: Finding 1: cli.ex:23 and :607 show --auto-approve but OptionParser at :102 parses --auto-approve-all. User gets unknown flag error. Finding 2: Issues.create/1 defaults priority to 2 at issues.ex:225; spec 6.2 requires agent-created default of 3. IssueCreate tool works around it but GenServer contract is wrong. Rejected compaction ordering: if compact_closed_issues fails to write, it returns original issues; detect_and_fix_cycles writing those back is consistent with what's on disk (no data corruption). Missing features (deft work, dep commands) already in work list.

summary: Confirmed dispatch error mapping bug; command descriptions sent to review
reasoning: SlashCommand.dispatch maps all load_definition errors to :not_found at slash_command.ex:98-99. This is wrong type at API boundary — callers can't distinguish missing vs broken skills. Filed as work item. Command descriptions: spec section 4.2 shows descriptions but section 1.1 prohibits frontmatter. Spec is internally contradictory. Sent to specd_review.md. Rejected dead error branch (registry.ex:271): empty file gets :invalid_yaml_format instead of :empty_file, but behavior is correct (reject empty file).

summary: ETS :public vs :protected sent to review; hardcoded lead_id already tracked
reasoning: ETS is :public at store.ex:170 but spec requires :protected. Code has a legitimate reason (async load task writes from separate process). Ambiguous whether to fix code or update spec — sent to specd_review.md. Hardcoded 'main' lead_id in tool_runner.ex:157 is already covered by the existing 'Implement per-Lead cache isolation' work item.

summary: No new findings — all features already tracked in work list
reasoning: Agent found entire git strategy unimplemented and config fields missing. All are already covered by existing work items in specd_work_list.md (job branch creation, per-Lead worktrees, merge strategy, squash-merge, worktree cleanup, orphan cleanup, resolve_git_root fix). Config fields are implicit dependencies of these items.

summary: Confirmed 2 findings: global atom registration and byte_size token estimation
reasoning: Finding 1: start_link uses name: __MODULE__ (global singleton) at rate_limiter.ex:145. Per orchestration spec, RateLimiter is per-job under Job.Supervisor. Second job crashes. Finding 2: estimate_message_tokens uses byte_size at rate_limiter.ex:280,292,299 instead of String.length. Unlike OM tokens.ex which self-calibrates, rate_limiter uses fixed @chars_per_token=4, so multi-byte overcounting is not compensated. Rejected starvation promotion finding: process_queue re-inserts with the promoted queue_key from take_smallest, preserving the promotion correctly.

summary: No new findings beyond existing work items; RateLimiter global name filed under rate-limiter
reasoning: Agent found entire orchestration layer missing (Foreman, Lead, Runner) and Config missing job keys — all already tracked in work list. RateLimiter global name is filed under rate-limiter v0.1 since the bug is in rate_limiter.ex. Config keys are implicit dependencies of existing orchestration work items.

summary: Confirmed 3 findings: is_buffering_reflection stuck, validate_sections rejects unknown sections, continuation hint wrong condition
reasoning: Finding 1: should_activate_reflection? at state.ex:1228 doesn't check is_buffering_reflection, allowing concurrent reflector spawn that orphans the buffered task and permanently sticks is_buffering_reflection=true. Finding 2: validate_sections at parse.ex:220 returns error on unknown sections; spec section 3.5 says ignore. fallback_parse loses current_task/continuation_hint. Finding 3: build_continuation_hint at context.ex:215 checks Enum.any?(observed_message_ids) but spec 5.3 requires checking whether messages were actually trimmed. Rejected 1 finding: byte_size vs String.length in tokens.ex — calibration_factor self-calibrates via EMA against actual LLM token counts, absorbing the byte_size/char difference.

summary: Confirmed: ResultStore.load/1 corrupt line drops entire run from export
reasoning: A single corrupt JSONL line causes load/1 to return {:error, reason} for the whole run. export/1 silently filters failed loads via Enum.filter. Confirmed at result_store.ex:99-110 and :172-176. Rejected two other agent findings: RegressionDetection key mismatch (eval.compare module doesn't exist, only atom-keyed paths reach infrastructure_failure?) and Baselines nil soft_floor (save/1 always writes numeric values, nil only possible via manual corruption).

summary: Fixed buffered reflection serialization guard in maybe_activate_buffered_chunks
reasoning: Added `not state.is_buffering_reflection` check to line 1055 guard. Previously only checked `not state.is_reflecting`, allowing observation chunks to activate while buffered Reflector is running. This wasted LLM calls when stale reflection was discarded by epoch check. Per spec section 6.4, Observer and Reflector must not run concurrently — the check must prevent activation during both active reflection and reflection buffering.

summary: Updated observational-memory spec section 9: OM persistence uses separate `<session_id>_om.jsonl` snapshot file, not session JSONL entries
reasoning: Review finding identified spec/code divergence — spec incorrectly stated OM persists as session JSONL entries, but code correctly uses separate file to avoid write interleaving when session and OM systems write concurrently. Separate file approach was deliberate (per specd_history.md). Updated spec to match implementation. Added verification work item to confirm implementation aligns with updated section 9.

summary: Confirmed: issues_compaction_days config not passed to GenServer
reasoning: cli.ex:76 calls Issues.start_link() with no args. Config value for issues.compaction_days is never passed. GenServer always defaults to 90 days.

summary: Confirmed: --auto-approve-all flag registered as --auto-approve
reasoning: cli.ex:97 registers auto_approve: :boolean (maps to --auto-approve). Spec section 8 requires --auto-approve-all. Wrong flag name at CLI boundary.

summary: Rejected: deft work, dep add/remove, work.cost_ceiling already in work list
reasoning: These are known unimplemented features already tracked as blocked work items. No new findings.

reasoning: tool_runner.ex:108-132 runs maybe_spill_to_cache on all tool results. If use_skill definition exceeds threshold, agent.ex:1405-1413 injects cache stub as system message instead of actual skill definition.

summary: Rejected: command descriptions missing from system prompt
reasoning: Commands have no description field in their format (no frontmatter). The spec example may be illustrative. Code correctly lists what's available. Not functionally wrong.

reasoning: store.ex:170 uses :public so async load task can write during init. Access control is enforced via GenServer API. Code works correctly, just uses a different pattern. Comment explains the rationale.

reasoning: Load task matches :ok which is what load_dets_to_ets returns — works correctly. cache_read returns string errors through tool system which renders as text regardless. No callers pattern-match on :miss/:expired atoms.

reasoning: Orchestration is largely unimplemented but all items already tracked. Config fields and supervisor wiring are implicit in existing implementation work items.

reasoning: specd_history.md records this as a deliberate choice to avoid JSONL write interleaving. Spec section 9 says session JSONL. Code works correctly with separate file. Sent to specd_review.md for human decision.

summary: Confirmed: buffered reflection does not block observation activation
reasoning: state.ex:1052-1059 checks not state.is_reflecting but not not state.is_buffering_reflection. Observations can activate while buffered Reflector runs. Epoch check prevents data corruption but wastes an LLM call.

reasoning: prompt.ex:269 outputs [Image attachment] not [Image: filename.png] as spec section 3.3 requires. Filename data is lost at Observer input boundary.

summary: Marked Implemented — no findings
reasoning: All agent-reported findings were either cosmetic (idle symbol), owned by other specs (slash command implementations), or expected stubs (orchestration features). TUI spec scope explicitly excludes slash command implementations. The dispatch mechanism works correctly.

summary: Confirmed: missing Makefile targets, safety hard-fail, CI workflows
reasoning: test.eval.e2e and test.eval.benchmark Makefile targets missing (spec section 3.1). Safety 90% hard-fail not enforced (section 3.2). No CI workflow files. Rejected eval.compare error handling finding — agent misread code (both paths return 3-element tuple).

summary: Implemented system-level skill injection for slash commands
reasoning: Per spec section 2.4, skills must be injected as system instructions, not user messages. Added Agent.inject_skill/2 function and updated TUI to use it for slash command skill invocation. Commands continue to use user messages as specified. Both agent-initiated (use_skill tool) and user-initiated (slash command) skill invocations now correctly inject as system messages.

summary: Implemented token calibration from provider usage events
reasoning: Modified Observer and Reflector to capture Usage events from provider streams; updated collect_stream_text_loop to track usage data alongside text accumulation; threaded usage through Observer/Reflector return values; added calibrate_from_usage/3 helper to OM.State that calls Tokens.calibrate/3 with exponential moving average (alpha=0.1) when output_tokens > 0; wired calibration into Observer and Reflector result handlers; calibration updates calibration_factor in state and sets snapshot_dirty flag per spec section 7

summary: Implemented /correct slash command following /forget pattern
reasoning: Created both command file and tool following same two-mode (search/confirm) pattern as /forget tool; appends CORRECTION marker with 'Replace "[old]" with "[new]"' format per spec section 11

summary: Implemented /forget command with two-mode tool (search + confirm)
reasoning: Created Deft.Tools.Forget with mode="search" to find matches and mode="confirm" to append CORRECTION markers. Appended corrections to User Preferences section which uses append merge strategy. Added append_correction/2 to OM.State to handle marker insertion.

summary: Fixed JudgeCalibration.load_latest_result/1 to return {:error, :not_found} when directory doesn't exist
reasoning: When the calibration directory doesn't exist, File.ls/1 returns {:error, :enoent} which falls through the with clause. Added else clause to convert {:error, :enoent} to {:error, :not_found} per the docstring, maintaining semantic consistency - callers don't need to distinguish between 'directory missing' and 'no matching files'

summary: Fix extract_prompt_text to handle both Deft.Message.Text structs and serialized maps
reasoning: extract_prompt_text/1 pattern matched only %{type: "text", text: text} but Entry.Message content can contain Deft.Message.Text structs (which lack :type field). Added pattern match for %Deft.Message.Text{text: text} before the map pattern to handle both cases, making the function robust to both serialized JSON and struct formats.

decision: Fixed make test.eval.holdout target to use --only holdout instead of --only eval --only holdout. ExUnit treats multiple --only flags as OR logic, so the original command ran all eval tests instead of just holdout tests. The spec (section 1.4) requires holdout tests to be excluded from regular eval runs and only executed via make test.eval.holdout.

Implemented /observations slash command as a tool (Deft.Tools.Observations) + command file (.deft/commands/observations.md). Tool supports three modes: summary (Current State + User Preferences + today's entries), full (complete dump), and search (filter by term). Chose to implement as a tool rather than inline command logic to allow programmatic access and better separation of concerns. Refactored execute/2 to reduce cyclomatic complexity per credo guidelines.

summary: Fix om_enabled config bypass to read from config struct
reasoning: agent/context.ex:90 and agent.ex:617 were reading om_enabled via Application.get_env(:deft, :om_enabled, true) but no code calls Application.put_env. Changed both to read from the config struct (Map.get(config, :om_enabled, true)) matching the pattern at agent.ex:1450. Updated function signatures: get_om_context/1 to get_om_context/2 (added config param), notify_om_messages_added/2 to notify_om_messages_added/3 (added config param). Updated all 5 call sites to pass config.

summary: Re-fetch OM context after sync fallback to get fresh observations
reasoning: check_sync_fallback triggers force_observe/force_reflect which update the OM state, but get_om_context was returning the pre-sync data fetched before the fallback. Solution: ignore first fetch result, call OMState.get_context again after check_sync_fallback completes to get freshly observed/reflected data.

summary: Found 4 findings: sync fallback stale context, om_enabled config bypass, missing slash commands, missing token calibration
reasoning: Sync fallback calls force_observe/force_reflect but returns pre-sync data. om_enabled reads from Application env instead of config struct. /forget, /correct, /observations commands are spec-required but absent. Tokens.calibrate/3 exists but is never called.

summary: Rejected findings: image filenames (no filename in struct), current_task injection (spec says no separate injection)
reasoning: Image struct only has media_type and data fields — no filename available. Spec line 252 explicitly says current-task is folded into Current State section with no separate injection.

summary: Found 2 findings: OM event wrapper mismatch, extract_prompt_text pattern match failure
reasoning: OM state broadcasts {:om_event, {:om, ...}} but TUI matches {:om, ...} — events silently discarded. extract_prompt_text matches %{type: "text", text: text} but Message.Text has no :type field — always returns empty string.

summary: Found 2 findings: holdout make target OR logic, judge calibration error type mismatch
reasoning: make test.eval.holdout uses --only eval --only holdout which is OR in ExUnit, running all eval tests. JudgeCalibration.load_latest_result returns {:error, :enoent} instead of {:error, :not_found} when calibration directory missing.

summary: Found 1 finding: slash command skill injection as user message instead of system instruction
reasoning: TUI chat.ex sends skills via {:submit, full_text} (user message) instead of injecting as system-level instruction per spec section 2.4. Code has TODO acknowledging this. Other findings rejected: system message collapsing is correct API behavior, use_skill tool result 'Skill loaded' matches spec mechanism.

summary: No new findings — all gaps already tracked in work list
reasoning: deft work, dep add/remove CLI, --auto-approve-all flag all already tracked. Relative path in resolve_file_path works correctly in practice. Compaction before cycle detection is correct — is_ready? treats compacted deps as satisfied.

summary: No new findings — ETS :public is defense-in-depth, per-Lead cache isolation already tracked
reasoning: ETS :public vs :protected is a pattern choice, not functional bug. Load task error path is extremely unlikely edge case. Hardcoded 'main' lead_id already tracked. Session.Worker registry issue belongs to sessions spec.

summary: No new findings — entire system unimplemented but fully tracked in work list
reasoning: All orchestration features (Foreman, Lead, Runner, job lifecycle) already tracked. Store terminate/2 double-shutdown claim rejected — do_cleanup correctly sets closed:true in GenServer state.

summary: No new findings — 429 handling, adaptive concurrency, cost tracking already tracked; byte_size heuristic and starvation FIFO are not bugs
reasoning: Findings 1-3 already in work list. byte_size vs String.length is a reasonable heuristic choice. Starvation promotion preserves FIFO within priority groups via gb_trees key ordering.

summary: No new findings — entire spec unimplemented but fully tracked in work list
reasoning: All git-strategy features already tracked in specd_work_list.md. Only existing code is Deft.Git behaviour for path resolution.

summary: Fixed memorizing... status never visible by not clearing om_sync_fallback on observation_started
reasoning: OM state.ex broadcasts :sync_fallback (sets om_sync_fallback: true) then immediately :observation_started (was setting om_sync_fallback: false). Events process before any render, so memorizing... indicator never displays. Fixed by removing om_sync_fallback: false from :observation_started, :reflection_started, and :buffering_started handlers; only clear it on :observation_complete and :reflection_complete when OM work finishes.

summary: Added user message to assigns.messages on submit
reasoning: User messages were never displayed in chat view because the submit handler called Deft.Agent.prompt/2 and cleared input without adding a user message to assigns.messages. Fixed by creating a %{role: :user, content: text, timestamp: DateTime.utc_now()} message and appending it to assigns.messages before calling Deft.Agent.prompt/2, following the same pattern used for assistant and system messages. This makes the existing render_message/2 function for :user role reachable.

summary: Fixed Reflector compress_with_retry to respect max 2 LLM calls
reasoning: The code was making a 3rd LLM call when both level 0 and level 1 attempts exceeded the target. Changed attempt_compression to return {:retry, compressed, level} instead of {:retry, level}, so the second attempt's output can be accepted directly without a 3rd call. This ensures compliance with spec section 4.3's maximum of 2 LLM calls.

summary: Fixed keep_tail/3 non-contiguous window bug
reasoning: Enum.reduce skips oversized messages but continues iterating, allowing older smaller messages after gaps. Replaced with Enum.reduce_while to halt on first message exceeding budget, ensuring contiguous tail (same pattern as truncate_session_history_to_target/4 fix).

summary: Added tool_result blocks for successful use_skill calls
reasoning: Anthropic API requires every tool_use block in assistant messages to have a corresponding tool_result in the next user message. Previously, use_skill success only generated system messages with skill definitions but no tool_result blocks, causing 400 errors. Fixed by generating simple tool_result blocks ("Skill loaded") for use_skill alongside the system message injection.

summary: Fixed timestamp/0 to remove fractional seconds
reasoning: DateTime.to_iso8601/1 produces microsecond precision but spec requires no fractional seconds per section 2; applied DateTime.truncate(:second) before to_iso8601 to match spec format contract

summary: Persist cycle fixes to disk during init/1
reasoning: detect_and_fix_cycles was clearing dependencies in memory but never persisting. Cycles would be detected and 'fixed' on every restart with repeated warnings. Modified to track whether cycles were found and persist corrected issues using write_issues_during_init, matching the pattern used by compact_closed_issues.

summary: Use :public ETS mode instead of :protected for async loading compatibility
reasoning: Spec requires both :protected mode and Task.async for DETS loading, but these are incompatible. Task.async spawns separate process that cannot write to :protected ETS. Changed to :public mode to enable async loading. Access control still enforced via GenServer write/4 API.

summary: Found user messages never displayed in chat view
reasoning: Agent submit path calls Deft.Agent.prompt/2 and clears input but never adds user message to assigns.messages. render_message/2 for :user role exists but is unreachable. Confirmed by code review of chat.ex:316-331.

summary: Found memorizing... status indicator never visible
reasoning: OM state.ex broadcasts :sync_fallback immediately followed by :observation_started (lines 312-314). TUI handles :sync_fallback by setting om_sync_fallback: true but :observation_started immediately clears it before any render cycle.

summary: Found keep_tail/3 produces non-contiguous message window
reasoning: Enum.reduce skips oversized messages but continues iterating, allowing older smaller messages after a gap. Same bug pattern as truncate_session_history_to_target/4 which was already fixed. Needs Enum.reduce_while.

summary: Found Reflector compress_with_retry makes 3 LLM calls exceeding spec max of 2
reasoning: After two {:retry, level} results, code makes a 3rd call_llm_for_compression at line 132-141. Spec section 4.3 explicitly caps at 2 LLM calls.

summary: Found missing tool_result for successful use_skill calls causes API 400
reasoning: use_skill_success_results are split from regular_results but no ToolResult block is generated for them. Anthropic API requires every tool_use to have a matching tool_result. Next API call will fail with 400.

summary: Found Store DETS loading regression: handle_continue blocks instead of Task.async
reasoning: init/1 uses handle_continue(:load_dets) which blocks GenServer. Spec and history both say Task.async should be used so GenServer is ready immediately. Code has regressed from the specified pattern.

summary: Found detect_and_fix_cycles not persisted to disk
reasoning: detect_and_fix_cycles in init/1 modifies in-memory issues but never writes corrected list back to JSONL file. Cycles reappear on every restart.

summary: Found DateTime.to_iso8601 produces fractional seconds breaking doctest
reasoning: DateTime.utc_now() returns microsecond precision. to_iso8601/1 includes fractional seconds but doctest regex expects no fractional seconds. Need DateTime.truncate(:second).
